title: Bonthaina nodifera Riedel 2024, comb. n.
author: Riedel, Alexander
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero

steps.instruction: Bonthaina nodifera (Voss), comb. n. Phraotes nodifer Voss 1940: 282–284. Type locality: SW Sulawesi, Mt. Lompobatang. Description . (Fig. 10a). Length. Pronotum plus elytron 4.72 mm. Integument black; with sparse appressed yellowish transparent scales. Head with sulcus separating rostral base curved; vertex with radial furrows deeply entrenched. Eyes asymmetrically convex, drawn posteriad. Rostrum shorter than wide (0.70 X), sides slightly converging; enlarged apical declivity subglabrous, contrasting rugose sculpture of head. Pronotum 1.09 X wider than long; coarsely punctate-nodulose. Elytra subglobose 1.16 X as long as wide; tuberculate; tubercles nodulose, with sparse erect scales. Genitalia. (Fig. 10b). Material examined. INDONESIA, S-Sulawesi: 1 female: Mt. Lompobatang, Malino, 1800 m, E 119° 53.517´S 05° 17.833´, 13-14-VII-2001; genitalia in glycerol in microtube; DNA extraction ARC7254 (GenBank # OR557455) (SMNS). Notes . The type specimens could neither be located at the Zoological Museum Bogor (Cibinong, Indonesia) and the Naturalis Biodiversity Center (Leyden, Netherlands), the two most likely depositories. However, a single female specimen from the type locality can be assigned with high confidence to this species.
